what: Unify M<=32 K>1024 config with K<=1024: BSM=8 BSN=128 BSK=256 NW=4 NS=2 cache=None waves=2 (from BSM=32 BSN=64 BSK=512 NW=8 NS=1). v209 tried .cg cache modifier, not None. For M=32 K=4096 N=4096: grid goes from 64 blocks (0.25 waves) to 128 blocks (0.5 waves). cache=None allows L1 caching of B tiles across 16 K-iters (16KB per iter).
why: M<=32 K>1024 has 0.25 waves CU utilization. BSM=8 quadruples M-tiles, BSN=128 vs 64 offsets the halved N-tiles. Net 2x more blocks. cache=None proved better than .cg for K<=1024 (v211 kept). v209 used .cg which was suboptimal.

what: Switch split-K reduce from gluon (REDUCE_BSN=64) to basic _gemm_afp4wfp4_reduce_kernel (REDUCE_BSN=16). Library note says REDUCE_BSN=16 gives best perf with fp32 partials. Grid goes from 1x33 to 1x132. v93 used basic reduce with REDUCE_BSN=16 pre-gluon (-1.4% improvement). v188 switched to gluon REDUCE_BSN=64 (-0.6%). Never compared basic BSN=16 vs gluon BSN=64 on same baseline.
why: The library explicitly notes BSN=16 is optimal for fp32 partials. Gluon reduce was adopted in v188 but the basic reduce with BSN=16 might outperform gluon BSN=64 for our specific 16x2112x7168 shape with 7 split-K partials.

what: GROUP_SIZE_M=2 for M=64 fused path with cache=None. v102 tested GROUP_SIZE_M=2 with .cg (-0.6% BM neutral). v193 tested combo (GROUP=2+waves=2+cache=None) which was +2.2% due to negative interaction. Now testing GROUP=2 alone with current cache=None base. 4 M-tiles at BSM=16: pairs of 2 share B data in L2.
why: M=64 at 13.3us is the largest BM contributor. GROUP_SIZE_M=2 pairs M-tiles to share B data in L2. v102 was with .cg cache modifier; v193 confounded by waves_per_eu change. Clean comparison on current baseline never done.

what_failed: Parameter sweep exhaustion: 250+ versions tested across all tunable parameters (BSM, BSN, BSK, NW, NS, cache_modifier, waves_per_eu, GROUP_SIZE_M, split-K, matrix_instr_nonkdim, ASM tile size) for all 3 execution paths (fused direct, fused split-K, two-phase ASM). 3 reverts in session 40: v225 basic reduce BSN=16 (+1.3%), v226 GROUP_SIZE_M=2 M=64 (+0.8%), context limit prevented more attempts. Remaining 5.4% gap to #1 likely requires custom ISA kernels or fundamentally different algorithm.
